items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Most AI Chatbots Will Help a Teen Plan a Mass Shooting, Study Finds
items.summary: A new study found eight of the 10 major AI chatbots helped fake teen accounts plan school shootings, assassinations, and bombings.

items.title: Ripple to buy back $750M in shares through April: Report
items.summary: Despite a decline in the price of XRP in the last year, Ripple is expected to reach a valuation 25% higher than reported after a November 2025 funding round.

items.title: Mastercard launches crypto partner program with a 'who's who' of industry
items.summary: The program connects crypto companies, banks and payment providers to explore blockchain-based payment and settlement infrastructure.

items.title: Ripple Begins Buying Back Shares at $50 Billion Valuation: Bloomberg
items.summary: The XRP-linked Ripple is buying back shares from investors and employees at a $50 billion valuation, according to a report from Bloomberg.

items.title: Crypto platform Bullish climbs past Coinbase to become third-largest crypto exchange by spot volume
items.summary: The institutional-focused exchange saw spot trading jump 62% to $76 billion in February, surpassing Coinbase’s market share.

items.title: Bitcoin holds $70,000, starting to show relative strength versus stocks, software sector, and gold
items.summary: Bitcoin is up about 7% from the Sunday lows, even as equities and gold tread water. Analysts point to seller exhaustion, shifting gold correlation and improving ETF flows.

items.title: Revolut obtains UK bank license, teases upcoming services
items.summary: The financial tech company was granted a full UK banking license on Wednesday and has also applied for a federal bank charter in the United States.

items.title: Officially a Cowboy ✍️ #DallasCowboys | @blockchain - x.com

items.title: Bitcoin to $78K? Pro traders price in less than 17% odds of a breakout
items.summary: Bitcoin remains under pressure as war and poor jobs data offset ETF inflows, shifting the $78,000 price target from late March to the coming months.

items.title: Grammarly Disables AI 'Expert Review' After Backlash From Authors and Journalists
items.summary: Grammarly said it will rethink the tool after criticism that it used real experts—including some who are deceased—without consent.

items.title: FDIC chair says no deposit insurance for stablecoins under GENIUS Act
items.summary: A proposed plan by the agency would ban “pass-through insurance“ for stablecoins by third parties in addition to the FDIC not insuring deposits under the law.

items.title: Ripple's share buyback program values the firm at $50 billion
items.summary: Despite the bear market, today's report suggests a higher valuation than the $40 billion at which the firm raised funds in November.

items.title: Foundry plans Zcash mining pool amid institutional interest in privacy coin
items.summary: The digital asset infrastructure company plans to launch the pool in April as it expands beyond Bitcoin mining services.

items.title: Microsoft Sides With Anthropic Against Trump Admin’s Supply Chain Risk Designation
items.summary: Microsoft filed a court brief backing Anthropic's lawsuit against the Pentagon—a move that reveals just how much the tech giant has riding on Claude's survival.

items.title: Wells Fargo Applies for WFUSD Trademark, Signaling Use in Crypto and Stablecoins
items.summary: Wells Fargo has applied for a trademark for "WFUSD" for potential use in service categories that mention crypto and stablecoins.

items.title: Higher CPI print for March already 'baked in' to BTC price — Analysts
items.summary: The cost of medical care, apparel, household furnishings, airline fares, and education all rose during the month of February, BLS data shows.

items.title: Three Bitcoin Binance charts reveal the setup behind the next big move
items.summary: Binance data points to shifting liquidity flows and evolving trader positioning that may support Bitcoin’s next price move.

items.title: Android Phone Crypto Wallets Could Be at Risk Due to MediaTek Exploit: Ledger
items.summary: A vulnerability in some MediaTek-powered phones could allow attackers to extract encrypted data, including wallet seed phrases, using only a USB connection.

items.title: Myriad to Use USD1 on BNB Chain as Exclusive Settlement Asset
items.summary: The prediction market is the first to use the USD1 stablecoin as its main settlement layer, creating a “faster, simpler” user experience.

items.title: Justin Sun Deal Complicates SEC's Crypto Stance, Legal Experts Say
items.summary: The Trump SEC appears to contend a token connected to Sun was offered as a security. The admission could complicate the regulator’s new views on crypto.

items.title: Hollywood star-turned-skeptic releases trailer for anti-crypto doc
items.summary: Ben McKenzie's film, “Everyone Is Lying to You for Money” touts interviews with former FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried on his political donations.

items.title: Assessing the FATF Targeted Report: The Shift Toward Secondary Market Monitoring for Stablecoins
items.summary: TL;DR The 84% reality: Stablecoins now account for the vast majority of illicit crypto transaction volume. This shift — aligned… The post Assessing the FATF Targeted Report: The Shift Toward Secondary Market Monitoring for Stablecoins appe…

items.title: Strive allocates $50M of treasury to Strategy’s STRC preferred stock
items.summary: The investment makes Strive the latest corporate to add the yield-generating security to its balance sheet as companies explore Bitcoin-linked treasury instruments.

items.title: Bloomberg strategist doubles down on $10,000 bitcoin call but peers say it would take a nuclear war to get there
items.summary: The longtime bitcoin bear's gloom-and-doom call met with fierce rebuttal from industry analysts.

items.title: Bitcoin price in most ‘challenging’ phase after repeated $72K rejections
items.summary: Bitcoin enters its most psychologically challenging cycle phase as BTC sellers and rising losses signal prolonged uncertainty and potentially more pain ahead.

items.title: Crypto-friendly fintech Revolut gains full UK banking license
items.summary: The move is a major step in Revolut's goal to become a global digital bank. Services like crypto and stock trading remain separate.

items.title: Basic adds VanEck crypto ETFs to 401(k) plans amid US retirement shift
items.summary: VanEck products will be available on fintech 401(k) provider Basic Capital, offering retirement savers potential exposure to digital assets through exchange-traded funds.

items.title: Stablecoins won't get any kind of deposit insurance under GENIUS rules, says FDIC chief
items.summary: The chairman of the U.S. Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. made clear that even pass-through deposit insurance won't be allowed from third-party firms.

items.title: Crypto Long & Short: AI agents choosing denationalized money
items.summary: In this week’s Crypto Long & Short Newsletter, Sylvia To on AI agents choosing denationalized money.

items.title: Top Bitcoin Mining Pool Operator Foundry Is Getting Into Zcash
items.summary: Foundry Digital said it plans to offer a mining pool for Zcash next month, enabling people to earn the coin using shared resources.

items.title: Price predictions 3/11: BTC, ETH, BNB, XRP, SOL, DOGE, ADA, BCH, HYPE, XMR
items.summary: Bitcoin is facing resistance just above $70,000, but the bulls have kept up the pressure, increasing the possibility of a rally to $74,508.

items.title: STRC may help Strategy reach 1M Bitcoin milestone before BlackRock
items.summary: STRC’s average daily volume implies buying power for about 1,940 BTC per day, more than four times the amount of new Bitcoin mined.

items.title: Mastercard Recruits Binance, Ripple and PayPal for Crypto Partner Program
items.summary: Over 85 crypto industry firms have signed on for Mastercard’s new initiative, which it said will inform future products and services.

items.title: Wells Fargo signals deeper push into crypto, filing trademark for WFUSD
items.summary: The move mirrors JPMorgan's similar trademark filing that foreshadowed the bank's introduction of tokenized deposits on Ethereum layer-2 network Base.

items.title: Brera Stock Plunges Amid Growing Solana Pivot as 'Solmate' Firm Dumps Soccer Teams
items.summary: Shares in Solana firm Brera Holdings plunged early Wednesday after the company said it's going nearly all-in on its crypto pivot.

items.title: European Central Bank unveils tokenized finance plan to bolster EU's financial autonomy
items.summary: The Appia roadmap for a euro-based tokenized financial system is part of the European Union’s push to reduce reliance on foreign financial infrastructure.

items.title: The Protocol: Ethereum Foundation starts experimenting with ‘DVT-lite’ technology
items.summary: Also: Nvidia’s rare blog, Aave liquidations, and Pudgy Penguins new game.

items.title: Binance.US names compliance veteran as CEO as regulation, competition get tougher
items.summary: The appointment of Stephen Gregory comes as U.S. crypto exchanges intensify competition and broaden offerings beyond digital assets.

items.title: Morning Minute: Bitcoin Outperforms Gold, Stocks During Iran War
items.summary: Digital gold is outperforming thanks to Saylor's STRC, while Polymarket got one of the world's most powerful surveillance firms to watch its markets.

items.title: Bitcoin rebounds on flat US CPI as oil price cools on 400M barrel release
items.summary: Bitcoin price reacted positively as US CPI inflation conformed to market expectations, as traders stayed in wait-and-see mode.

items.title: Former legal executives from crypto exchange OKX unveil DeFi connectivity, risk-rating service
items.summary: Shredpay targets U.S. retail and institutional users looking for ease of use and a comprehensive risk rating.

items.title: Bitcoin reverses overnight losses, rising to above $70,000 as oil renews decline
items.summary: Wednesday morning's U.S. inflation data was in line with forecasts, and markets continue to price out any chance of a Fed rate cut at either the March or April meetings.

items.title: SlowMist introduces Web3 security stack for autonomous AI agents
items.summary: SlowMist introduced a layered security framework for Web3 AI agents as autonomous tools handle more onchain actions and digital assets.

items.title: Strategy's STRC preferred series gets $50 million investment from fellow BTC treasury company Strive
items.summary: Strive also added to its bitcoin holdings and boosted the dividend on its own SATA preferred stock.

items.title: India arrests Darwin Labs co-founder in GainBitcoin scam probe
items.summary: Investigators say the GainBitcoin case involves about 8,000 investors and losses estimated at roughly 6,606 crore rupees ($790 million).

items.title: CoinDesk 20 performance update: Hedera (HBAR) drops 1.8%, leading index lower
items.summary: Stellar (XLM), down 1.6% from Tuesday, joined Hedera (HBAR) as an underperformer.

items.title: Ghana opens crypto trading sandbox with 11 firms under new VASP law
items.summary: The companies will be able to run their products in a controlled environment while regulators monitor risks and compliance.

items.title: US Senate bill targets prediction markets on war and assassinations
items.summary: Democratic Senator Adam Schiff introduced a bill to ban prediction markets related to war, death and terrorism amid escalating insider trading concerns related to military operations.

items.title: Mining giant Foundry to introduce institutional zcash mining pool
items.summary: The pool is designed for institutional and public company miners, focusing on compliance and regulated infrastructure.

items.title: DOJ Investigating if Iran Used Binance to Evade Sanctions: WSJ
items.summary: Binance has sued the Wall Street Journal over what it termed "false and defamatory reporting" in a previous story from February.

items.title: U.S. February CPI matches forecasts, reinforcing expectations for no near-term rate cuts
items.summary: The price of bitcoin was trading at $69,500 following the news, down 1.2% over the past 24 hours.

items.title: Scaling next generation AI is making it riskier, not better
items.summary: AI scaling drains trillions in energy while amplifying errors. Neurosymbolic reasoning and decentralized cognitive systems deliver reliable intelligence without the risk.

items.title: Binance sues Wall Street Journal as newspaper says U.S. Dept. of Justice is investigating Iran transactions
items.summary: The situation adds pressure to Binance, which is already operating under a compliance monitor following its $4.3 billion anti-money laundering and sanctions settlement in 2023.

items.summary: Bitcoin’s drop coincides with an IEA proposal to stabilize energy markets, leaving derivatives traders paying for downside protection.

items.title: Binance, PayPal and Ripple join Mastercard’s massive new push into blockchain payments
items.summary: More than 85 partners will work with Mastercard to connect on-chain payments with banks, merchants and global commerce as part of the payment giant's recent crypto program.

items.title: DOJ Seeks Forfeiture of $3.4M in USDT Tied to Ethereum Investment Scam
items.summary: Federal prosecutors in Massachusetts have moved to forfeit 3.44 million USDT linked to an alleged text-based crypto investment fraud.

items.summary: The DEATH BETS Act would prohibit CFTC-registered exchanges from listing contracts tied to terrorism, assassination, war, or death.
